calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment
MINI MINUTES: Appearances via Zoom:
Kimberly Chu, Esq. of Brayton Purcell, Tel. 415.898.1555, appearing for the Plaintiff
Jennifer Willis, Esq. of

The Court's tentative ruling directed the parties to appear.

Matter called. Ms. Willis states that the parties entered into a stipulation to set aside the default and that the stipulation and order was submitted to the court this morning for efiling. Discussions are held.

At the conclusion of discussions, the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant Key Air Conditioning Co.'s Motion to Set Aside Default and Default Judgment is HEARING REQUIRED. Non-Opposition filed by Plaintiffs. Counsel for Plaintiffs and Defendant are ordered present. Counsel may appear in person or remotely via zoom.

Defendant did not demonstrate grounds for the Court to invoke its inherent, equitable power to set aside a default judgment on the ground of extrinsic fraud or mistake.

Specifically, Defendant's Notice of Motion states the motion is based on Code of Civil Procedure Section 473(b), however, Defendant fails to assert any "mistake, inadvertence, surprise, or excusable neglect." Defendant also fails to include the proposed pleading with its motion. Despite this, the motion primarily relies on the argument that extrinsic factors prevented Defendant from adequately presenting a defense.

Judge: Rochelle C. East. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Matter Not Reported. Court Reporter: Bailiff: Not Present. (301/RCE)

MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Amber Rae, Esq., of ARMSTRONG & ASSOCIATES, LLP (510-433-1830) appearing for defendant / moving party Exxon Mobil Corporation
Oren P. Noah, Esq., of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party James Kearney

RULING:
DEFENDANT EXXON MOBIL CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Exxon Mobil Corporation's motion for summary judgment is denied. Mr. Manwill's declaration creates a triable issue whether Mr. Kearney was exposed to asbestos-containing insulation in debir that was disturbed by defendant. Defendant's objections to Mr. Manwill's declaration are overruled. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: Scott Hughes, CSR # 12365 = (220/HEK)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jan-06-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Todd A. Duplanty, Esq., of SELMAN BREITMAN LLP (415-979-2070), appearing for defendant / moving party Oakfabco, Inc.
Oren P. Noah, Esq., of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party James Kearney

RULING:
DEFENDANT OAKFABCO, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Oakfabco Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that the gaskets and refractory material removed from Kewanee boilers were original factory parts. Defendant's separate statement did not include sufficient facts to show that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that none of the original factory parts of the Kewanee boilers that Mr. Kearney was exposed to were asbestos-free. Moreover, Mr. Kasparian's deposition testimony (submitted by plaintiffs) creates a triable issue whether those parts contained asbestos. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: Scott Hughes, CSR # 12365 = (220/HEK)

Off Calendar Dec-30-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Jasun C. Molinelli, Esq., of ARCHER NORRIS (925-930-6600), appearing for defendant / moving party Cahill Contractors, Inc.
Oren P. Noah, Esq., of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties James Kearney, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT CAHILL CONTRACTORS, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Cahill Contractors, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Kearney was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. The deposition testimony of Mr. Kearney and Mr. Cantley taken in this case preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 = (220/EHG)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Dec-30-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Oren P. Noah, Esq., of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties James Kearney, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT REDWOOD PLUMBING CO., INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
No other appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Redwood Plumbing Co., Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issue 1 (negligence) and off calendar as to issues 2, 3 and 4 (strict liability, false representation and punitive damages). Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Kearney was exposed to asbestos-containing insulation inserts attributable to defendant. Mr. Hixson's deposition testimony precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Even if defendant did sustain its burden, Mr. Hixson's deposition testimony and declaration create a triable issue whether Mr. Kearney was exposed to asbestos-containing insulation inserts attributable to defendant. Defendant's objections to Mr. Hixson's declaration are overruled. Plaintiffs have dismissed strict liability, false representation and punitive damages. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 = (220/EHG)
